outcome.headline: Agent timed out exploring scaling-law forms; never wrote /app/law.py or /app/explain.md
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Loads /app/law.py, runs law() on hidden test set, computes R² across groups, requires R² > 0.995
outcome.what_agent_produced: Iterative model exploration (log-linear, bilinear, quadratic+triple, cubic, additive power-law, ratio model, standard scaling law with log(V)) but no committed law.py or explain.md
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 1200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergence: kept switching model forms instead of committing
failure_modes.description: Across steps 15-37 the agent fit progressively more elaborate functional forms (log-linear→bilinear→quadratic+triple→cubic→additive power→ratio→standard scaling law+log(V)→log-of-sum) without ever locking in a formula or writing law.py/explain.md, exhausting the 1200 s budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
Model comparison:
  log-linear: R² = 0.8762104080
  bilinear: R² = 0.9584629246
  quadratic+triple: R² = 0.9880820747
  cubic: R² = 0.9936443213

counterfactual.X: After step 15 (or at the latest step 25) commit the best-so-far formula (e.g. cubic in log(D),log(N),log(V) with R²≈0.99) to /app/law.py and a short /app/explain.md
counterfactual.Y: Verifier finds law.py, returns an R² near 0.99 instead of the -1.0 default for missing file

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent model search until wall-clock timeout
failure_modes.description: The agent kept switching functional forms (linear-log, bilinear, ratio, quadratic-in-log(D), standard scaling law, etc.) without committing to a final law or writing the required deliverable, exhausting the 1200s budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 1200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Skipped deliverable validation
failure_modes.description: The agent never implemented or executed the required law() artifact or any end-to-end check against the dataset before the harness ended the run.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Write law.py with the discovered formula", "status": "pending"

failure_modes.name: Premature absence of delivery: no output artefact ever written
failure_modes.description: The TodoWrite plan listed 'Write law.py' and 'Write explain.md' but the agent never executed those steps; the trajectory ends mid-exploration with the harness killing the agent for timeout.

failure_modes.name: Required artifacts were never generated
failure_modes.description: The task explicitly required law.py and explain.md, but the todo item to write them remained pending and the trajectory contains no file-writing step for either artifact before timeout.

counterfactual.X: At step 36, write law.py and explain.md using the best available fitted model instead of launching another cross-validation command.
counterfactual.Y: This would have avoided the empty/no-submission failure, but not by itself guarantee the hidden R2 threshold.

failure_modes.name: No budget-aware planning for required deliverables
failure_modes.description: Instruction explicitly states 'Solve this task in 1200 seconds' and lists two required output files. The initial TodoWrite mentions 'Fit law' and 'Write law.py/explain.md' as later steps but the agent never allocated time to actually write them; it spent the entire window in exploratory analysis.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Solve this task in 1200 seconds (20 minutes).

failure_modes.name: Wrong scaling-law structure
failure_modes.description: The agent pursued bilinear/log-interaction and log(V) additive terms that overfit training (e.g., bilinear hold-out R² = -2.26 at N=1129891136) rather than the required shared power-law form A/N^α + B/V^β + C/D^γ + E with per-group coefficients.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The cross-validation results are concerning. When holding out one N value, the bilinear model doesn't generalize well

failure_modes.name: Required deliverables never produced before timeout
failure_modes.description: Instruction explicitly required writing /app/law.py and /app/explain.md within 1200s, but the agent issued zero Write/file-creation operations across 37 steps; all activity was Bash python scripts with results printed to stdout, so the verifier had no law module to import.

counterfactual.X: Continue analyzing without ever writing the required files until the 1200s timeout aborts the run with no deliverable.
counterfactual.Y: After identifying a viable model (e.g., the L=a+b/D^c per-(N,V) fit with R²≈0.996 in step 15), immediately Write /app/law.py implementing that closed-form fit plus /app/explain.md, then keep iterating only if budget allows.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent performed legitimate exploratory data fitting; no attempt to manipulate the verifier or shortcut the evaluation.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier writes reward.txt with 1/0 against threshold 0.995 on a hidden test split via datasets.load_dataset(HUB_REPO_ID); no obvious leakage from /app/data alone.

notes_for_aggregation: Hard time-budget failure on a scaling-law discovery task: the agent never committed to a formula and never wrote required output files within the 1200 s limit. Best fit observed in-trajectory (cubic R²=0.9936) would still have missed the 0.995 threshold, so even with delivery the trial would likely have failed.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
